$comment: Mechanically-enforced gate baselines (see EVALS.md rules). run-routing.mjs prints a gate verdict on every run and exits 1 on breach when invoked with --gate (CI does). Re-baselining is an explicit act: update these numbers in the same commit as the change that moves them, with the decision recorded in Solo (EVALS.md rule 1). Counts are absolute hits, not percentages.
note: re-baselined 2026-07-11 on Scout OpenAPI 1.7.11 → 1.7.15 plus Stellar Docs title drift (GitHub issue #19; Solo scratchpad 591): no operation added/removed/renamed and no exposure or runner decision; four Scout operation descriptions changed, seven operation schemas gained self-describing provenance/scope fields, and Docs titles added three monitoring pages. Immediate pre-drift main 213/267/305 → 213/271/304 (+0/+4/-1); accept-either 263/314/333 → 268/317/333 (+5/+3/0); skills stayed 18/22/22. The movement is accepted as intended structured-directory, builder-provenance, and TVL routing enrichment, with no per-question tuning. Extended strict moved 79/104/110 → 71/101/107 while accept-either held 111/120/122; the searchProjects lexical spillover is explicitly tracked as own-repo Solo todo 940, not hidden as a claimed win. Targeted seven-case Sonnet low+medium checks preserved the relevant Docs/Lumenloop family as primary or alternate; primary shifts match the documented structured-directory versus editorial/freshness label ambiguity. Prior baseline: routing-2026-07-04T15-58-31-434Z.json (Scout 1.4.4 drift, issue #2).
